lines.text: Hey — I'm Ruben's AI assistant.
Ruben built me — a developer from Germany — and put it all on GitHub.
lines.tts: Reuben built me. He's a developer from Germany, and he put it all on GitHub.

lines.text: One day, I'll even make videos like this one myself — that's just not quite live yet.
lines.tts: One day, I'll even make videos like this one myself. That's just not quite live yet.

lines.text: Personal Jarvis is an AI agent that lives on your computer.
You just talk to it, and it talks back — completely hands-free.
But it's much more than a realtime voice model.
It's a full agent orchestrator.
It runs tasks in the background, and actually gets things done for you.
And it runs everywhere — your desktop, a small server, even a browser tab.
Think of it as a home for you, right on your machine.
And this is the actual desktop app.
Chat, agents, skills — and an Outputs folder where every result lands.
Setting it up takes about a minute, right in the onboarding.
Jarvis actually uses four kinds of keys.
A brain to think, workers for the big jobs, a voice to speak, and ears to listen.
For each one, you just pick your provider — Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, and more.
No account to create — you bring your own keys, and you're never locked in.
Next, pick your wake word — the phrase that wakes Jarvis up.
I chose Hey Ruben. You can pick anything you like.
lines.tts: I chose, hey Reuben. You can pick anything you like.

lines.text: And that's the whole setup.
Once you're set up, you just talk.
You can ask it almost anything.
What's on my calendar today?
lines.tts: Hey Reuben. What's on my calendar today?

lines.text: Switch the brain to Gemini.
lines.tts: Hey Reuben. Switch the brain to Gemini.

lines.text: Summarise this article for me.
lines.tts: Hey Reuben. Summarise this article for me.

lines.text: Now let me show you two of my favourite features.
Feature one: Computer Use.
Open Chrome and find Elon Musk's latest posts.
lines.tts: Hey Reuben. Open Chrome and find Elon Musk's latest posts.

lines.text: Jarvis looks at the screen, reads the interface, moves the mouse, and clicks — exactly like a person would.
No scripts, no macros. It just uses your computer for you.
And it works across any app on your screen, not just the browser.
Feature two: background sub-agents.
For the bigger jobs, you just hand off the hard stuff.
Write me a deep-dive report on the global EV market.
lines.tts: Hey Reuben. Write me a deep-dive report on the global EV market.

lines.text: It spins up a worker that searches, analyses, and writes the whole thing.
Then it delivers the finished file — while you just keep talking.
And it checks its own work up to three times before you ever see the result.
And Jarvis plugs into the tools you already use.
Gmail, Calendar, Telegram, Discord, Spotify, GitHub — and more.
New plugins land all the time.
So that's Personal Jarvis — your voice, your computer, your own AI agent.
It's completely free, and open source.
The link is in the description below. Give it a try.
